title: Dodda Basaveshwara Temple
tagline: Dodda Basaveshwara Temple — spiritual town of Karnataka

overview.short: The Dodda Basaveswara temple is situated in the town of Kurugodu, which is now a taluk/divisional headquarters in the Ballari district of Karnataka, India.
overview.description: The Dodda Basaveswara temple is situated in the town of Kurugodu, which is now a taluk/divisional headquarters in the Ballari district of Karnataka, India. The Dodda Basaveswara temple ("temple of the big bull-lord") is the main attraction in town; it attracts many pilgrims who are devotees of Shiva. The idol of the big bull, called Nandi or Nadeeswara, is cut out of a monolithic rock, and is 12 ft (3.7 m) high. The annual Ratha Yatra of the deity is conducted on the Holi Purnima day, which marks the beginning of the spring season, as well as the beginning of the New Year.Every year Ratha Maho

topPlaces.name: Kurugodu
topPlaces.description: Kurugodu is a town in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is headquarters of Kurugodu taluk in Bellary district of Karnataka.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Dodda Basaveshwara Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Dodda Basaveshwara Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Dodda Basaveshwara Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Bellary Airport (21 km) and the nearest railway station is Hubballi Junction (182 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Dodda Basaveshwara Temple?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Dodda Basaveshwara Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Dodda Basaveshwara Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Dodda Basaveshwara Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.
